excerpt: Army review used Korean witness statements to place more than 400 refugees at Im Gae Ri before movement toward No Gun Ri.
transform_note: Used as a floor, not a total, for civilian person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Source says 'over 400', so 400 is a minimum anchor.

geographic_scope: Twin tunnels / double railroad underpass near No Gun Ri
excerpt: The Army review records an eyewitness estimate that nearly 300 people were already inside the twin tunnels before others entered.
transform_note: Used as a tunnel-density anchor for the civilian exposure range, not as the total refugee count.
confidence_note: Single-witness estimate; useful as a density floor rather than a complete population count.

geographic_scope: No Gun Ri double railroad overpass vicinity
excerpt: Army review places 2/7 Cavalry around the overpass on 26 July and 1/7 Cavalry in the vicinity from the afternoon of 26 July onward.
transform_note: Used to bound military person-months; no direct troop count is given.
confidence_note: Presence is well supported; exact present-for-duty strengths are not.

excerpt: Official Peace Park/local-government search snippet reports 218 recognized victims including 150 dead, 13 missing, and 55 disabled/wounded.
pages_or_sections: Search-result snippet for official No Gun Ri Peace Park page
transform_note: Used as the conservative low direct-death floor; missing are kept separate.
confidence_note: This is a documentary-certification floor, not a complete victim census.

excerpt: Official certification snippet distinguishes 13 missing from the 150 dead.
pages_or_sections: Search-result snippet for official No Gun Ri Peace Park page
transform_note: Excluded from the core direct-death estimate path.
confidence_note: Missing status is explicit and should not be silently folded into deaths.

excerpt: National Archives of Korea summary preserves the joint-investigation statement that 248 casualties were reported to Yeongdong County Office.
pages_or_sections: National Archives topic page, lines 304-305 and 309
transform_note: Kept outside the core direct-death path because the source does not separate dead, wounded, and missing here.
confidence_note: Useful contextual casualty ceiling/floor but not a clean death count.

geographic_scope: No Gun Ri railroad tracks and bridge area
excerpt: Hanley summarizes the Peace Park account as a combined U.S. air-and-ground killing with an estimated death toll of 250-300.
transform_note: Used for best/high death scenarios; this is a secondary synthesis of the Peace Park narrative, not the original certification file.
confidence_note: Higher than the certification floor because it likely includes uncertified dead with no surviving paperwork.

excerpt: TRCK narrative states that 120 households and about 600 residents lived on Wolmido in September 1950.
transform_note: Used as the resident civilian denominator anchor; additional laborers are treated separately in uncertainty notes.
confidence_note: Resident count appears explicit; it may not include temporary laborers from Incheon.

excerpt: TRCK narrative says residents left the village when bombing resumed on 12 September and never returned.
transform_note: Used to limit civilian person-month exposure to roughly 10-12 September rather than the full 10-15 September military episode.
confidence_note: Exact hour of departure is unknown, so the denominator uses scenario bounds.

excerpt: TRCK narrative reports a Korean intelligence estimate of about 400 KPA on Wolmido, later confirmed through prisoner interrogation.
transform_note: Used as the garrison anchor for military person-months.
confidence_note: This likely captures island defenders better than the inflated 1,000-person U.S. intelligence figure that folded civilians into the enemy count.

excerpt: USMC aviation history explicitly places 3/5 Marines in the assault on Wolmido.
transform_note: Used as the anchor for including a directly assaulting ground-force component in military person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Unit identity is clear; exact assault strength is modeled because the source does not state a headcount.

excerpt: TRCK decision summary states that while only 10 named victims were identified, actual victims are estimated at around 100 when unidentified/dispersed families are considered.
transform_note: Used as the resident-civilian death anchor; additional laborers are handled in the estimate range rather than in this claim.
confidence_note: Rounded estimate; named-victim count is much lower because identification is incomplete.

excerpt: TRCK narrative states that a considerable number of Incheon-area laborers on the island were also killed.
transform_note: Used only to widen the high civilian death scenario above the ~100 resident-victim anchor.

excerpt: TRCK decision summary records 95 napalm tanks dropped and strafing of the east side of Wolmido.
transform_note: Contextual attack-intensity indicator only; not used directly in DR arithmetic.
confidence_note: Strong operational detail from official finding.

excerpt: USMC aviation history gives 108 counted enemy dead on Wolmido.
transform_note: Used as the low military direct-death floor.
confidence_note: Counted dead only; does not exhaust possible fatalities from bombarded caves/emplacements.

excerpt: USMC aviation history adds a lower-bound statement that at least 150 more enemy were entombed in caves and emplacements.
transform_note: Used to widen the best/high military death scenarios; the figure is a lower bound derived from prisoner interrogation rather than direct body count.
confidence_note: Operationally valuable but less secure than the 108 counted dead.

excerpt: Sea Services history confirms 17 Marines wounded on the objective.
transform_note: Auxiliary injury metric only; not part of core DR.
confidence_note: Cross-checks the Marine-side casualty figure reported elsewhere.
